ead.archdesc:
======= RMA02993Laurence D. Stifel papers14-27-2993Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
May 20042B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:09:18 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Laurence D. Stifel papers
Stifel, Laurence D., 1930-1995.
14-27-2993
11 cubic feet. 11 cubic feet.
[ca.1970-1994]. Correspondence, notes, professional papers, and subject files. Correspondence, notes, professional papers, and subject files relating to Stifel's work in economics and agriculture, with particular reference to Africa, Nigeria, Thailand, and the Philippines. Collection material in English 3320111
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, May 2004
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Development economist. Secretary of the Rockefeller Foundation, 1974-1984; Director-General of the International Institute for Tropical Development in Ibadan, Nigeria, 1985-1990; Visiting Professor in Cornell University Southeast Asia Program and the Cornell International Institute for Food, Agriculture, and Development, 1991-1995.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, notes, professional papers, and subject files relating to Stifel's work in economics and agriculture, with particular reference to Africa, Nigeria, Thailand, and the Philippines.
Cite As:
Laurence D. Stifel papers, #14-27-2993. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Agriculture -- Economic aspects -- Thailand. Agriculture -- Economic aspects -- Philippines. Agriculture -- Economic aspects -- Nigeria. Agriculture -- Economic aspects -- Africa. Cornell University. Southeast Asia Program Cornell University. Institute for Food, Agriculture, and Development
